title: Rahul M. Shah | Fragomen, Del Rey, Bernsen & Loewy LLP
content:
Fragomen in Matawan, NJ
Fragomen, Del Rey, Bernsen & Loewy, LLP
90 Matawan Road, 4th Floor
Fragomen in Matawan, NJ
Fragomen, Del Rey, Bernsen & Loewy, LLP
90 Matawan Road, 4th Floor
Fragomen in Matawan, NJ
Fragomen, Del Rey, Bernsen & Loewy, LLP
90 Matawan Road, 4th Floor
Rahul utilizes his extensive immigration law experience to help clients build compliant global mobility programs, increase efficiencies and improve their employees’ experience. Whether they are building a first-time program or expanding an existing one, Rahul develops customized strategies based on the company’s size, industry, employee population and ensures processes and outcomes are clear and understandable to all stakeholders. He leads Fragomen’s Matawan, NJ office and serves as a member of the firm’s IT Consulting Group and Pro Bono Ambassadors network.
Rahul focuses on clients within a variety of industries, including software development, audit, tax and advisory services, human capital management solutions, consumer products, e-commerce, chemicals and energy, assisting them with a wide range of nonimmigrant visa matters, as well as permanent residency matters involving PERM labor certification applications, complex immigrant worker petitions, and multinational executive and manager petitions. He also provides counsel on the immigration implications of corporate mergers and acquisitions, outlining the impact on the foreign national employees as part of the due diligence process and devising a plan to facilitate business continuity from an immigration perspective before and after the restructuring. Additionally, Rahul regularly advises clients on U.S. immigration compliance, Labor Condition Application (LCA) regulations, and USCIS anti-fraud efforts.
As clients navigate complex regulatory, policy and economic changes, they know they can rely on Rahul’s clear understanding of the impact of these factors on their business and guidance on how best to implement necessary processes that will save costs, retain and move the right talent around the globe, while maximizing revenue.
Responsiveness, empathy and a hands-on approach are the cornerstones of Rahul’s client service—one that has won him many client accolades throughout his career. He prides himself on being able to correctly harness the full scope of Fragomen’s worldwide capabilities depending on the clients’ needs, aligning solutions with the client’s unique culture, risk tolerance and business objectives. He has facilitated crucial meetings and discussions between clients and U.S. Consulates abroad to increase the understanding of the process as they move their workforce around the world.
Coming from a family of immigrants, Rahul has a personal connection to the process, but more importantly he knows that his role helps multiple stakeholders—from the corporation to the foreign national and their family—benefit the economy and the country.
Prior to joining Fragomen, Rahul served as a Staff Attorney with the U.S. Department of Justice, Board of Immigration Appeals through the Attorney General’s Honors Program.
Rahul speaks English and Gujarati.

- American University, Washington College of Law, J.D., cum laude , 2002
- University of Michigan, B.A., 1999
Bar Admissions: District of Columbia
Practice Limited to Federal Immigration & Nationality Law
Named to Chambers USA , 2020 to 2025
In 2000, Rahul was awarded the Anheuser-Busch Norman Y. Mineta Fellowship by the National Asian Pacific American Bar Association Educational Fund (AEF) for his public interest work with the American Immigration Law Foundation (AILF).

### Professional accomplishments
Member, South Asian Bar Association
Member, American Immigration Lawyers Association
Member, Princeton Mercer Regional Chamber of Commerce
Member, New Jersey Relocation Council
Member, The Indus Entrepreneurs (TiE), New Jersey Chapter
Board Member, American University Washington College of Law, Philadelphia Alumni Chapter
Volunteer, American Friends Service Committee (AFSC)
Facilitated senior-level meetings between corporate clients and the U.S. Embassy in New Delhi, the U.S. Consulate in Chennai and the U.S. Embassy in Dublin to discuss the L-1B specialized knowledge visa category, which ultimately strengthened the L-1B visa programs of clients in industries such as software development and audit, tax and advisory services.
During the COVID-19 pandemic, provided clients with a comprehensive analysis of the changes and business impact resulting from the immigration-related Presidential Proclamations and Travel Bans. Led multiple Town Hall calls regarding the Proclamations for the foreign national employee populations of multinational clients, one of which was attended by over 900 employees of the company worldwide.
Successfully advocated on behalf of a client to register the client’s major U.S. operating companies as E-1 treaty trader entities with the U.S. Consulate in Frankfurt, enabling the client to utilize the E-1 work visa category for qualified employees/candidates that are international transfers and short-term assignees to the United States. Worked closely with the client to strategize, prepare and present the necessary supporting corporate documentation to demonstrate to the U.S. Consulate that the client’s services and technology result in substantial trade between Germany and the United States, resulting in an expeditious and positive outcome for the client’s Global Mobility and U.S. immigration programs.

### Publications and lectures
Speaker, Philadelphia Area Relocation Council (PARC) Fall Summit 2025, Philadelphia, PA, 9 October 2025.
Quoted, “ Guiding Legal Pathways: Navigating the Complexities of Business Immigration Law .” The Advocate , Pages 32-33, Summer/Fall 2025.
Speaker, “Industry Update and a Look Forward: Immigration, Tax, Real Estate.” Philadelphia Area Relocation Council (PARC) Fall Summit 2024 , Glen Mills, PA, 10 October 2024.
Speaker, “On the Brink: Global Mobility Strategies in an Economic Downturn.” South Asian Bar Association of North America (SABA – NA) 2023 Annual Conference , Boston, Massachusetts, 22 July 2023.
Speaker, “How Healthy is Your Mobility Program? Recovering from Immigration Headaches.” New Jersey Relocation Council 2017 Spring Conference , Somerset, New Jersey, 4 April 2017.
Speaker, “New Administration: New Impact on Immigration.” Forum for Expatriate Management (FEM) New Jersey Chapter Meeting , Jersey City, New Jersey, 23 March 2017.

## Explore more at Fragomen
Fragomen earned top national and regional rankings in Chambers USA 2026, including its 13th consecutive year in Band 1 nationwide.
Senior Manager Samantha Arnold examines the remaining opportunities under Ireland’s winding-down Immigrant Investor Programme and what they mean for eligible investors and their families.
Government Affairs Strategy Director Shuyeb Muquit analyses the UK’s latest net migration decline, exploring what the data reveals about policy impact, economic trade‑offs and the risks of over‑correction in future migration strategy.
Partner Bo Cooper discusses the practical considerations facing employers and foreign nationals amid recent changes to green card processing.
In this #FragomenFC episode, Partner Rick Lamanna, Senior Manager Sergio Flores and Senior Associate Jake Paul Minster discuss final travel and immigration considerations ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
Senior Manager Samantha Arnold discusses Ireland’s citizenship framework and the gaps that can remain following reforms to birthright citizenship.
In this video, Partner Diana Quintas discusses how Brazil’s family reunion visa supports long-term international assignments and outlines key eligibility, documentation and compliance considerations for employers and families relocating to Brazil.
Attorney María José Clarke explores Chile’s intensified immigration enforcement framework under President José Antonio Kast, examining new employer compliance obligations, work authorization rules for foreign nationals and the legal and financial consequences of unauthorized employment.
Senior Manager Jonathan Hill examines how the UK’s new RAG rating system and stricter compliance metrics could shape universities’ approaches to international student recruitment and sponsorship compliance.
Knowledge Management Director Ana Sofia Walsh and Senior Client Engagement Manager Soraya Driessen examine the European Commission’s EU Inc. proposal and its potential to reshape corporate structures across the EU, highlighting the resulting complexities and unresolved questions for immigration, work authorization and cross-border workforce mobility.
